creator: fbsd@www.zefox.net
text:
It turns out that when stray characters get into the serial console
traffic it's possible to backspace over the Login: prompt. I'm not 
clear on the significance, but it's a departure from normal. 

In the case noticed, after logging into the terminal server, su-ing
to root and running tip ucom to pick up the USB-serial adapter and
monitor the client's serial console there came up something like

Login: #@&)*%$#

Hitting backspace repeatedly erased not only the stray characters,
but also the Login: prompt, which isn't possible normally, and can't
be repeated after hitting the enter key.

Ucom in this case is
ucom:dv=/dev/cuaU0:br#115200:pa=none:
in /etc/remote. The same entry is used on four P2v1.1,
two Pi3 and one Pi4. So far only the Pi3s exhibit the
behavior.
